vignette.name: VOID Link Audio
vignette.p: VOID Link Audio is a Max package that brings Ableton Link Audio streaming directly into the Max patcher.

It exposes two MSP objects, void.linkaudio.send~ and void.linkaudio.receive~, that let any Max patch publish and receive audio over the local Link Audio network alongside Ableton Live, TouchDesigner, VCV Rack, and any other Link Audio host on the same LAN.
Built on top of Ableton Link Audio (MIT license, by Ableton AG). Not endorsed by Ableton.
The core idea behind VOID Link Audio is simple and explicit:

ul.li: any application publishes one or more named audio channels
any application subscribes to a named audio channel

vignette.p: Channels are identified by name and discovered automatically over the local network. Audio flows in real time, sample-accurate, and in lock-step with the shared Ableton Link tempo and beat.

Publishes one or two channels of audio onto the network under a user-defined channel name. The number of inlets is fixed at creation time via the @stereo attribute (@stereo 1 for two inlets, @stereo 0 for one). The advertised channel is named via @channel and tagged with the local @peer name.

Subscribes to an audio channel published on the network and outputs the received signal on its left and right outlets. The target is addressed by @frompeer (the source peer's name) and @fromchannel (the channel name on that peer). Subscription is automatic and resilient: when the polling clock is enabled, the object continually retries to match the requested target against currently advertised channels and transparently reconnects after peer drops.
Every Link Audio participant on the network is identified by two pieces of information:

ul.li: a peer name, advertised once per process
one or more channel names, advertised per stream

vignette.p: A subscriber identifies its target by the pair (frompeer, fromchannel). Specifying frompeer is essential when multiple peers publish a channel with the same name. Channel names are case-sensitive.

Within a single Max instance, the local peer identity is shared across all VOID Link Audio objects (Link Audio uses a single shared peer name per process). On the Send side this identity is set via @peer. On the Receive side, no local peer name is exposed: the Receive only cares about the source's name via @frompeer.

Both objects ship with an internal Max clock that periodically re-applies their state. This makes the network connection robust against transient setup issues and naturally handles peers appearing and disappearing on the network.
The clock is controlled by two attributes:

ul.li: @poll 0|1 enables or disables the clock
@pollinterval <ms> sets the tick period (50 to 60000 ms)

vignette.p: With @poll 1 (the default), there is generally no need to manage subscriptions or retries by hand.
Both objects expose a status dictionary on their dumpout. The dictionary contains a complete snapshot of the object state, including:

ul.li: connection flags (enabled, audio_enabled, subscribed/publishing)
peer count and the list of channels currently visible on the network
stream parameters (sample rate, channel count)
traffic counters (frames received/published, drops, no-buffer events)
Link timing values (tempo, beat, phase, quantum)

vignette.p: A fresh dictionary is dumped automatically whenever a meaningful state change occurs (subscription gained/lost, peer count changed, channel list changed) and on demand by sending the info message. Editing attributes does not spam the dumpout.
Route the dictionary through dict.view for inspection, dict.unpack for fan-out, or route for selective handling.
VOID Link Audio is fully compatible with any host that implements the Ableton Link Audio protocol, including:

ul.li: Ableton Live (Link Audio In / Link Audio Out track inputs)
TouchDesigner (Link Audio Send / Receive CHOPs)
VCV Rack (Void Link Audio Send / Receive modules)
any VST3 / AU host (Structure Void's VoidLinkAudio plugins)
any other Max patch using the same package

vignette.p: Streams are exchanged on the local network with sample-accurate timing. Audio published from one host can be received by an arbitrary number of subscribers simultaneously.
VOID Link Audio aims to be:

ul.li: explicit — you choose what to publish, you choose what to receive
resilient — auto-poll handles transient network state silently
observable — the dumpout dictionary always reflects current truth
portable — the same idea, the same names, across hosts

vignette.p: The package is the Max-side counterpart of a broader VOID Link Audio effort that also targets TouchDesigner, VCV Rack, openFrameworks, and plug-in formats.

ul.li: void.linkaudio.send~ — publish audio to a Link Audio channel
void.linkaudio.receive~ — receive audio from a Link Audio channel
